source.title: Shoulder Pain: Diagnosis, Treatment and Referral Guidelines for Primary, Community and Intermediate Care
source.population: Adults with common shoulder conditions in primary, community, or intermediate care.
source.setting: Primary, community, and intermediate musculoskeletal care.
source.exclusions: Exact for shoulder red flags, broad assessment, subacromial pain, frozen shoulder, glenohumeral arthritis, and instability; international and partial for a generic UAE shoulder-pain workflow.

exact_location.heading: Frozen shoulder, glenohumeral arthritis, and instability summaries

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports range-of-motion, stiffness, function, instability, trauma, recurrent dislocation, and clinician-selected imaging or referral documentation across common shoulder presentations.

items.rationale: Supports clinician assessment of other shoulder presentations and referral context for frozen-shoulder follow-up; no clinical decision is generated.

items.final_wording: Requires direct clinician-patient decisions and lists infection, unreduced dislocation, tumour, traumatic acute cuff tear, and inflammatory disease as urgent concern contexts.

source.title: Shoulder Pain: Diagnosis, Treatment and Referral Guidelines for Primary, Community and Intermediate Care
source.population: Adults with common shoulder conditions in primary, community, or intermediate care.
source.setting: Primary, community, and intermediate musculoskeletal care.
source.exclusions: Exact for shoulder red flags, broad assessment, subacromial pain, frozen shoulder, glenohumeral arthritis, and instability; international and partial for a generic UAE shoulder-pain workflow.

exact_location.heading: Shared Decision Making and Urgent Red Flag Referrals for the Shoulder

source.evidence_paraphrase: Requires direct clinician-patient decisions and lists infection, unreduced dislocation, tumour, traumatic acute cuff tear, and inflammatory disease as urgent concern contexts.

items.rationale: Supports entered shoulder red flags for frozen-shoulder follow-up; no clinical decision is generated.
